title: Airport
description: Essential vocabulary and phrases for traveling through airports

video.title: Airport English - Check In, Security, Boarding

vocabulary.meaning: A card that allows you to board an airplane
vocabulary.example: Please show your boarding pass at the gate.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of registering at the airport
vocabulary.example: Online check-in opens 24 hours before departure.

vocabulary.meaning: The act of leaving a place
vocabulary.example: The departure time is 10:30 AM.

vocabulary.meaning: The act of reaching a destination
vocabulary.example: The arrival hall is on the ground floor.

vocabulary.meaning: Bags and suitcases used for traveling
vocabulary.example: Please collect your luggage at carousel 3.

vocabulary.meaning: The place where officials check goods entering a country
vocabulary.example: You must declare items at customs.

vocabulary.meaning: A building at an airport where passengers arrive and depart
vocabulary.example: International flights depart from Terminal 2.

vocabulary.meaning: An official document for international travel
vocabulary.example: Make sure your passport is valid for at least 6 months.

vocabulary.meaning: The area where passengers board the plane
vocabulary.example: Boarding will begin at gate B12.

vocabulary.meaning: A person who serves passengers on an airplane
vocabulary.example: The flight attendant offered drinks to passengers.

vocabulary.meaning: When a flight is late or postponed
vocabulary.example: The flight has a 2-hour delay due to weather.

vocabulary.meaning: A stop between connecting flights
vocabulary.example: We have a 3-hour layover in Tokyo.

vocabulary.meaning: Storage compartment above seats on a plane
vocabulary.translation: ngan chua hanh ly tren cao
vocabulary.example: Please store your bag in the overhead bin.

vocabulary.meaning: The passage between rows of seats on a plane
vocabulary.example: I prefer an aisle seat for more legroom.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of officially entering a country
vocabulary.translation: nhap cu / kiem tra ho chieu
vocabulary.example: Please join the immigration line for foreign passports.

vocabulary.meaning: The strip of ground where planes take off and land
vocabulary.example: The plane taxied to the runway.

vocabulary.meaning: A machine capable of flight
vocabulary.example: The aircraft departed on time.

vocabulary.meaning: The person who flies an airplane
vocabulary.example: The pilot announced a smooth flight ahead.

vocabulary.meaning: The team of people working on an aircraft
vocabulary.example: The crew welcomed passengers on board.

vocabulary.meaning: A small bag brought onto the plane cabin
vocabulary.example: Only one carry-on bag is permitted.

vocabulary.meaning: A safety strap worn around the waist on a plane
vocabulary.example: Please fasten your seat belt during takeoff.

vocabulary.meaning: A fold-down table attached to the seat in front on a plane
vocabulary.example: Please fold up your tray table for landing.

vocabulary.meaning: A seat on a plane next to the window
vocabulary.example: I always request a window seat.

vocabulary.meaning: The checkpoint where passports are checked
vocabulary.example: Queue up at passport control when you arrive.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of checking passengers and bags for dangerous items
vocabulary.example: Remove your shoes at security screening.

vocabulary.meaning: A form listing goods you are bringing into a country
vocabulary.example: Fill in the declaration form before landing.

vocabulary.meaning: Goods sold without local taxes at the airport
vocabulary.example: I bought perfume at the duty-free shop.

vocabulary.meaning: The area where passengers collect their checked bags
vocabulary.example: Your bags will be at baggage claim area B.

vocabulary.meaning: The moving belt that delivers luggage at an airport
vocabulary.example: Wait at carousel 4 for your bags.

vocabulary.meaning: A service that lets you hire a car at the airport
vocabulary.example: We booked a car rental for our road trip.

vocabulary.meaning: A comfortable waiting area for passengers at the airport
vocabulary.example: Business class passengers have lounge access.

vocabulary.meaning: The right to board the plane before other passengers
vocabulary.example: Premium ticket holders get priority boarding.

vocabulary.meaning: A premium seating section with more comfort on a plane
vocabulary.example: Business class seats are wider and more comfortable.

vocabulary.meaning: The standard lowest-priced class on a plane
vocabulary.example: We flew economy class to save money.

vocabulary.meaning: A covered walkway connecting the airport to an aircraft
vocabulary.example: We walked to the plane via the air bridge.

vocabulary.meaning: Airport workers who handle baggage and aircraft on the ground
vocabulary.example: The ground crew loaded the bags quickly.

vocabulary.meaning: The desk at the airport where you register for your flight
vocabulary.example: Go to the check-in counter two hours before departure.

vocabulary.meaning: An official permit to enter a foreign country
vocabulary.example: I need a tourist visa to visit that country.

vocabulary.meaning: The scheduled time to start boarding the plane
vocabulary.example: Boarding time is 30 minutes before departure.

vocabulary.meaning: A code that identifies a specific flight
vocabulary.example: Your flight number is VN423.

vocabulary.meaning: The hard surface of an airport where planes move
vocabulary.example: We waited on the tarmac before takeoff.

vocabulary.meaning: When an airplane leaves the ground and begins to fly
vocabulary.example: Passengers must stay seated during takeoff.

vocabulary.meaning: When an airplane comes down and touches the ground
vocabulary.example: The landing was smooth despite the bad weather.

vocabulary.meaning: A document giving you the right to travel on a flight
vocabulary.example: I booked my ticket online three months in advance.

vocabulary.meaning: Luggage that exceeds the weight limit and incurs extra charges
vocabulary.example: We had to pay for excess baggage at the airport.

vocabulary.meaning: An official message telling passengers to board their plane
vocabulary.example: Listen for the boarding announcement in the lounge.

vocabulary.meaning: A visa allowing short stay while connecting to another flight
vocabulary.example: You may need a transit visa for that stopover.

vocabulary.meaning: The path a plane takes from departure to destination
vocabulary.example: The flight route goes over the Arctic.

vocabulary.meaning: A company that provides air transport services
vocabulary.example: Which airline are you flying with?

vocabulary.meaning: Irregular air movements that cause a bumpy flight
vocabulary.translation: nhieu loan khi quyen / may bay bi xoc
vocabulary.example: Please fasten your seatbelt due to turbulence.

quiz.question: What document do you need to board an airplane?
Where do you collect your bags after landing?
What is the person who serves you on the plane called?
Where do officials check goods entering a country?
What is another word for bags and suitcases?
What do you call a stop between connecting flights?
What is the storage above seats on a plane?
